title: Add immersive 3D match view with Three.js (React Three Fiber)

requirements.text: Create a new CricketStadium3D component using React Three Fiber (@react-three/fiber) and @react-three/drei that renders an immersive 3D cricket stadium scene from an elevated broadcast-style camera angle. The scene must include: a flat green pitch strip in the center, an outfield ground plane with boundary rope marker, basic stadium stands around the boundary with crowd atmosphere (simple geometric shapes representing crowd tiers), and ambient + directional lighting with a warm floodlight-style glow. The camera should be positioned above and behind the batsman end, looking down the pitch toward the bowler end.
acceptanceCriteria: A 3D canvas renders a recognizable cricket ground with pitch strip, outfield, and boundary
Stadium stands/crowd geometry is visible around the boundary
Lighting gives a floodlit stadium atmosphere
Camera angle is elevated broadcast/isometric style looking down the pitch
Component renders without errors on all match pages

requirements.text: Add animated ball delivery trajectory to the CricketStadium3D component. For each new ball event emitted by the match simulator, animate a cricket ball (small sphere) traveling from the bowler end to the batsman end along a realistic arc (parabolic trajectory). The ball color should be red/white based on match format. The animation should complete within 1–1.5 seconds before the next delivery can begin. On wicket events, add a stumps-hit visual effect (stumps fly/scatter). On six events, animate the ball continuing over the boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Ball animates from bowler end to batsman end for each delivery
Animation follows a parabolic arc
Animation completes within 1.5 seconds
Wicket deliveries show a stumps-scatter effect
Six deliveries show ball trajectory continuing over the boundary

requirements.text: Add 11 fielder position markers to the CricketStadium3D scene. Each fielder is represented as a small colored cylinder or capsule on the ground. Fielder positions should be dynamically repositioned based on match situation: attacking field (more slips/gully) when batting team has few wickets lost, defensive field (spread outfield) when batting team is chasing a big total or batting team is strong. Pass current match state (wickets fallen, overs bowled, run rate) as props to drive fielder repositioning.
acceptanceCriteria: 11 fielder markers are visible on the 3D ground at all times
Fielder positions shift between attacking and defensive configurations based on match state
Markers are clearly distinguishable from the pitch/ground
Wicketkeeper position is always behind the stumps at batsman end

requirements.text: Integrate the CricketStadium3D component into the existing InternationalMatch.tsx match simulation screen. The 3D view should occupy the upper portion of the match screen (approximately 50–60% height) above the existing live scorecard (Scorecard component) and commentary feed (CommentaryFeed component). On mobile/small screens, the 3D view should be collapsible or reduced in height. The 3D view receives the current ball event and match state as props so animations trigger in sync with each 'Next Ball' or 'Auto-Simulate' step. Ensure no existing match simulation logic, backend calls, or scorecard functionality is broken.
acceptanceCriteria: 3D stadium view appears above the scorecard on the InternationalMatch screen
Ball animation triggers on each 'Next Ball' and during Auto-Simulate
Existing scorecard, commentary feed, and match logic remain fully functional
Layout is responsive — 3D view adapts to smaller screens
No regression in match result summary or navigation

requirements.text: Add stadium atmosphere particle/visual effects to the CricketStadium3D scene: a subtle crowd noise visual effect (rippling crowd wave animation as colored particles on the stands) triggered on six and wicket events, and floodlight cone effects (semi-transparent light shafts from four corner towers) that give the scene a night-match stadium feel. These effects should be lightweight and not cause frame rate drops below 30fps on average hardware.
acceptanceCriteria: Floodlight cone effects are visible from corner towers
Crowd wave particle effect triggers on six and wicket events
Visual effects do not cause noticeable frame rate degradation
Atmosphere effects are consistent with the dark/floodlit cricket theme

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths)
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui/ (immutable path)
All existing match simulation logic, scoring, and backend integration must remain intact
All existing tournament modes (IPL, BBL, Domestic, International) must continue to work without regression
Internet Identity authentication flow must not be affected
nonGoals: AAA-quality photorealistic graphics (Cricket 24 / Ashes Cricket level fidelity)
3D player character models with motion-captured animations
Integrating 3D view into IPL, BBL, or Domestic tournament pages (International match only for this iteration)
